paragraphs.en: In an essay entitled "Making It in America," the author Adam Davidson relates a joke from cotton country about just how much a modern textile mill has been automated: The average mill has only two employees today, "a man and a dog. The man is there to feed the dog, and the dog is there to keep the man away from the machines."
paragraphs.zh: 在一篇题为“在美国制造”的文章中，作者亚当·戴维森讲述了一个来自棉花之乡的笑话，讲述了现代纺织厂的自动化程度：如今，纺织厂平均只有两名员工，“一个人和一只狗。人在那里喂狗，狗在那里让人远离机器。”

paragraphs.en: Davidson's article is one of a number of pieces that have recently appeared making the point that the reason we have such stubbornly high unemployment and declining middle-class incomes today is largely because of the big drop in demand because of the Great Recession, but it is also because of the advances in both globalization and the information technology revolution, which are more rapidly than ever replacing labor with machines or foreign workers.
paragraphs.zh: 戴维森的文章是最近出现的众多文章之一，这些文章指出，当今失业率如此居高不下，中产阶级收入不断下降，很大程度上是因为大衰退造成的需求大幅下降，但也是因为全球化和信息技术革命的进步，机器或外国工人比以往任何时候都更快地取代劳动力。

paragraphs.en: In the past, workers with average skills, doing an average job, could earn an average lifestyle. But, today, average is officially over. Being average just won't earn you what it used to. It can't when so many more employers have so much more access to so much more above average cheap foreign labor, cheap robotics, cheap software, cheap automation and cheap genius. Therefore, everyone needs to find their extra — their unique value contribution that makes them stand out in whatever is their field of employment.
paragraphs.zh: 过去，拥有普通技能的工人，做着普通的工作，就能获得普通的生活方式。但是，今天，平均值正式结束。处于平均水平不会让你获得像以前那样的收入。当越来越多的雇主有更多机会获得高于平均水平的廉价外国劳动力、廉价机器人、廉价软件、廉价自动化和廉价天才时，它就不可能了。因此，每个人都需要找到自己的额外——独特的价值贡献，使他们在任何就业领域中脱颖而出。

paragraphs.en: Yes, new technology has been eating jobs forever, and always will. But there's been an acceleration. As Davidson notes, "In the 10 years ending in 2009, [U.S.] factories shed workers so fast that they erased almost all the gains of the previous 70 years; roughly one out of every three manufacturing jobs about 6 million in — total — disappeared."
paragraphs.zh: 是的，新技术一直在蚕食就业岗位，而且永远如此。但有一个加速。正如戴维森指出的那样，“在截至 2009 年的 10 年里，（美国）工厂裁员的速度如此之快，几乎抹去了过去 70 年的所有成果；大约每三个制造业岗位中就有一个（总共约 600 万个）消失了。”

paragraphs.en: There will always be change — new jobs, new products, new services. But the one thing we know for sure is that with each advance in globalization and the I. T. revolution, the best jobs will require workers to have more and better education to make themselves above average.
paragraphs.zh: 总会有变化——新的工作、新的产品、新的服务。但我们确信的一件事是，随着全球化和信息技术革命的每一次进步，最好的工作将要求工人接受更多更好的教育，以使自己高于平均水平。

paragraphs.en: In a world where average is officially over, there are many things we need to do to support employment, but nothing would be more important than passing some kind of G. I. Bill for the 2 l 8t century that ensures that every American has access to post­ high school education.
paragraphs.zh: 在一个平均水平正式结束的世界里，我们需要做很多事情来支持就业，但没有什么比通过某种 2-8 世纪退伍军人法案更重要的了，该法案确保每个美国人都有机会接受高中后教育。

questions.text: The joke in Paragraph 1 is used to illustrate ________
According to Paragraph 3, to be a successful employee, one has to ________
The quotation in Paragraph 4 explains that ________
options: gains of technology have been erased
job opportunities are disappearing at a high speed
factories are making much less money than before
new jobs and services have been offered

questions.text: According to the author, to reduce unemployment, the most important is ________
options: to accelerate the I. T. revolution
to ensure more education for people
to pass more bills in the 21st century

questions.text: Which of the following would be the most appropriate title for the text? ________

paragraphs.en: A century ago, the immigrants from across the Atlantic included settlers and sojourners. Along with the many folks looking to make a permanent home in the United States came those who had no intention to stay, and who would make some money and then go home. Between 1908 and 1915, about 7 million people arrived while about 2 million departed. About a quarter of all Italian immigrants, for example, eventually returned to Italy for good. They even had an affectionate nickname, "uccelli di passaggio," birds of passage.
paragraphs.zh: 一个世纪前，来自大西洋彼岸的移民包括定居者和寄居者。除了许多希望在美国永久定居的人之外，还有一些无意留下来、想赚点钱然后回家的人。 1908 年至 1915 年间，约有 700 万人抵达，约 200 万人离开。例如，大约四分之一的意大利移民最终永久返回意大利。它们甚至还有一个亲切的绰号“uccelli di passaggio”，即候鸟。

paragraphs.en: Today, we are much more rigid about immigrants. We divide newcomers into two categories: legal or illegal, good or bad. We hail them as Americans in the making, or brand them as aliens to be kicked out. That framework has contributed mightily to our broken immigration system and the long political paralysis over how to fix it. We don't need more categories, but we need to change the way we think about categories. We need to look beyond strict definitions of legal and illegal. To start, we can recognize the new birds of passage, those living and thriving in the gray areas. We might then begin to solve our immigration challenges.
paragraphs.zh: 今天，我们对移民的态度更加严格。我们将新来者分为两类：合法或非法、好或坏。我们称赞他们是正在成长中的美国人，或者将他们视为将被驱逐出境的外星人。这一框架对我们支离破碎的移民体系以及如何修复这一体系的长期政治瘫痪做出了巨大贡献。我们不需要更多的类别，但我们需要改变我们思考类别的方式。我们需要超越合法和非法的严格定义。首先，我们可以认识新的候鸟，那些生活在灰色地带并蓬勃发展的鸟。然后我们可能会开始解决移民挑战。

paragraphs.en: Crop pickers, violinists, construction workers, entrepreneurs, engineers, home health-care aides and physicists are among today's birds of passage. They are energetic participants in a global economy driven by the flow of work, money and ideas. They prefer to come and go as opportunity calls them. They can manage to have a job in one place and a family in another.
paragraphs.zh: 农作物采摘工、小提琴家、建筑工人、企业家、工程师、家庭保健助手和物理学家都是当今的候鸟。他们是由工作、金钱和思想流动驱动的全球经济的积极参与者。他们更喜欢随着机会的召唤而来来去去。他们可以设法在一个地方找到工作，在另一个地方建立家庭。

paragraphs.en: With or without permission, they straddle laws, jurisdictions and identities with ease. We need them to imagine the United States as a place where they can be productive for a while without committing themselves to staying forever. We need them to feel that home can be both here and there and that they can belong to two nations honorably.
paragraphs.zh: 无论是否获得许可，他们都能轻松地跨越法律、司法管辖区和身份。我们需要他们将美国想象成一个可以暂时高效工作而无需承诺永远留在美国的地方。我们需要他们感到这里和那里都可以有家，他们可以光荣地属于两个国家。

paragraphs.en: Accommodating this new world of people in motion will require new attitudes on both sides of the immigration battle. Looking beyond the culture war logic of right or wrong means opening up the middle ground and understanding that managing immigration today requires multiple paths and multiple outcomes, including some that are not easy to accomplish legally in the existing system.
paragraphs.zh: 为了适应这个人口流动的新世界，移民斗争双方都需要采取新的态度。超越正确或错误的文化战争逻辑意味着开放中间立场，并理解当今的移民管理需要多种路径和多种结果，包括一些在现有体系中不容易合法实现的目标。

questions.text: "Birds of passage" refers to those who ________
options: stay in a foreign country temporarily
leave their home countries for good

questions.text: It is implied in Paragraph 2 that the current immigration system in the US ________
options: should be adapted to meet challenges
has been fixed via political means

questions.text: According to the author, today's birds of passage want ________
options: the freedom to stay and leave

questions.text: The author suggests that the birds of passage today should be treated ________
The most appropriate title for this text would be ________

paragraphs.en: Scientists have found that although we are prone to snap overreactions, if we take a moment and think about how we are likely to react, we can reduce or even eliminate the negative effects of our quick, hard-wired responses.
paragraphs.zh: 科学家发现，虽然我们很容易做出过度反应，但如果我们花点时间思考一下我们可能会如何反应，我们可以减少甚至消除快速、固有反应的负面影响。

paragraphs.en: Snap decisions can be important defense mechanisms; if we are judging whether someone is dangerous, our brains and bodies are hard-wired to react very quickly, within milliseconds. But we need more time to assess other factors. To accurately tell whether someone is sociable, studies show, we need at least a minute, preferably five. It takes a while to judg e complex aspects of personality, like neuroticism or open -mindedness.
paragraphs.zh: 快速决策可能是重要的防御机制；如果我们要判断某人是否危险，我们的大脑和身体天生就会在几毫秒内迅速做出反应。但我们需要更多时间来评估其他因素。研究表明，要准确判断某人是否善于交际，我们至少需要一分钟，最好是五分钟。判断性格的复杂方面需要一段时间，比如神经质或开放性。

paragraphs.en: But snap decisions in reaction to rapid stimuli aren't exclusive to the interpersonal realm. Psychologists at the University of Toronto found that viewing a fast-food logo for just a few milliseconds primes us to read 20 percent faster, even though reading has little to do with eating. We unconsciously associate fast food with speed and impatience and carry those impulses into whatever else we're doing, Subjects exposed to fast-food flashes also tend to think a musical piece lasts too long.
paragraphs.zh: 但对快速刺激作出反应的仓促决定并不只存在于人际领域。多伦多大学的心理学家发现，尽管阅读与饮食无关，但只要看几毫秒的快餐标志，我们的阅读速度就会加快 20%。我们无意识地将快餐与速度和不耐烦联系在一起，并将这些冲动带入我们正在做的任何其他事情中。暴露于快餐闪光的受试者也倾向于认为音乐作品持续时间太长。

paragraphs.en: Yet we can reverse such influences. If we know we will overreact to consumer products or housing options when we see a happy face (one reason good sales representatives and real estate agents are always smiling), we can take a moment before buying. If we know female job screeners are more likely to reject attractive female applicants, we can help screeners understand their biases — or hire outside screeners.
paragraphs.zh: 然而我们可以扭转这种影响。如果我们知道当我们看到一张笑脸时我们会对消费品或住房选择反应过度（优秀的销售代表和房地产经纪人总是微笑的原因之一），我们可以在购买前花点时间。如果我们知道女性求职筛选人员更有可能拒绝有吸引力的女性求职者，我们可以帮助筛选人员了解她们的偏见，或者聘请外部筛选人员。

paragraphs.en: John Gottman, the marriage expert, explains that we quickly "thin slice" information reliably only after we ground such snap reactions in "thick sliced" long­ term study. When Dr. Gottman really wants to assess whether a couple will stay together, he invites them to his island retreat for a much longer evaluation: two days, not two seconds.
paragraphs.zh: 婚姻专家约翰·戈特曼（John Gottman）解释说，只有在我们将这种快速反应纳入“厚片”长期研究中之后，我们才能快速可靠地“薄片”信息。当戈特曼博士真正想评估一对夫妇是否会继续在一起时，他会邀请他们到他的岛屿静修所进行更长的评估：两天，而不是两秒钟。

paragraphs.en: Our ability to mute our hard-wired reactions by pausing is what differentiates us from animals: dogs can think about the future only intermittently or for a few minutes. But historically we have spent about 12 percent of our days contemplating the longer term. Although technology might change the way we react, it hasn't changed our nature. We still have the imaginative capacity to rise above temptation and reverse the high-speed trend.
paragraphs.zh: 我们通过暂停来抑制本能反应的能力是我们与动物的区别：狗只能间歇性地或几分钟地思考未来。但从历史上看，我们花了大约 12% 的时间来考虑更长远的问题。尽管技术可能会改变我们的反应方式，但它并没有改变我们的本性。我们仍然有想象力，能够超越诱惑，扭转高速发展的趋势。

questions.text: The time needed in making decisions may ________
options: predetermine the accuracy of our judgment
prove the complexity of our brain reaction
depend on the importance of the assessment
vary according to the urgency of the situation

questions.text: 0ur react10n to a fast-food logo shows that snap dec1s10ns ________
To reverse the negative influences of snap dec1s10ns, we should ________
John Gattman says that reliable snap reactions are based on ________
The author's attitude toward reversing the high-speed trend is ________

paragraphs.en: Europe is not a gender-equality heaven. In particular, the corporate workplace will never be completely family-friendly until women are part of senior management decisions, and Europe's top corporate-governance positions remain overwhelmingly male. Indeed, women hold only 14 per cent of positions on European corporate boards.
paragraphs.zh: 欧洲不是性别平等的天堂。特别是，除非女性参与高级管理决策，否则企业工作场所永远不会完全适合家庭，而欧洲企业最高治理职位仍然绝大多数是男性。事实上，女性在欧洲公司董事会中只占 14% 的职位。

paragraphs.en: The European Union is now considering legislation to compel corporate boards to maintain a certain proportion of women — up to 60 per cent. This proposed mandate was born of frustration. Last year, European Commission Vice President Viviane Reding issued a call to voluntary action. Reding invited corporations to sign up for gender balance goals of 40 per cent female board membership. But her appeal was considered a failure: only 24 companies took it up.
paragraphs.zh: 欧盟目前正在考虑立法强制公司董事会保留一定比例的女性成员——最高可达 60%。这项拟议的任务授权是出于挫败感。去年，欧盟委员会副主席维维安·雷丁呼吁采取自愿行动。雷丁邀请企业签署性别平衡目标，即董事会女性成员比例达到 40%。但她的上诉被认为失败了：只有 24 家公司接受了她的上诉。

paragraphs.en: Do we need quotas to ensure that women can continue to climb the corporate ladder fairly as they balance work and family?
paragraphs.zh: 我们是否需要配额来确保女性在平衡工作和家庭的同时能够继续公平地在企业晋升？

paragraphs.en: "Personally, I don't like quotas," Reding said recently. "But I like what the quotas do." Quotas get action: they "open the way to equality and they break through the glass ceiling," according to Reding, a result seen in France and other countries with legally binding provisions on placing women in top business positions.
paragraphs.zh: “就我个人而言，我不喜欢配额，”雷丁最近表示。 “但我喜欢配额的作用。”雷丁表示，配额得到了行动：它们“开辟了平等之路，突破了玻璃天花板”，这一结果在法国和其他制定了关于让女性担任高层商业职位的具有法律约束力的条款的国家中得到了体现。

paragraphs.en: I understand Reding's reluctance — and her frustration. I don't like quotas either; they run counter to my belief in meritocracy, governance by the capable. But, when one considers the obstacles to achieving the meritocratic ideal, it does look as if a fairer world must be temporarily ordered.
paragraphs.zh: 我理解雷丁的不情愿和她的沮丧。我也不喜欢配额；它们与我对精英统治、有能力者治理的信念背道而驰。但是，当人们考虑到实现精英理想的障碍时，看起来确实必须暂时建立一个更加公平的世界。

paragraphs.en: After all, four decades of evidence has now shown that corporations in Europe as well as the US are evading the meritocratic hiring and promotion of women to top positions — no matter how much "soft pressure" is put upon them. When women do break through to the summit of corporate power — as, for example, Sheryl Sandberg recently did at Facebook — they attract massive attention precisely because they remain the exception to the rule.
paragraphs.zh: 毕竟，四十年的证据表明，欧洲和美国的企业都在回避精英聘用和晋升女性担任高层职位的做法——无论她们受到多大的“软压力”。当女性确实突破到企业权力的顶峰时——例如谢丽尔·桑德伯格(Sheryl Sandberg)最近在 Facebook 所做的那样——她们会吸引大量关注，因为她们仍然是规则的例外。

paragraphs.en: If appropriate public policies were in place to help all women — whether CEOs or their children's caregivers — and all families, Sandberg would be no more newsworthy than any other highly capable person hvmg m a more Just society.
paragraphs.zh: 如果制定适当的公共政策来帮助所有女性——无论是首席执行官还是孩子的照顾者——以及所有家庭，桑德伯格不会比任何其他致力于建设更加公正社会的高能力人士更具新闻价值。

questions.text: In the European corporate workplace, generally ________
The European Union's intended legislation is ________
According to Reding, quotas may help women ________
The author's attitude toward Reding's appeal is one of ________
Women entering top management become headlines due to the lack of ________
